title: Business relief loans (April 2020 CARES Act / SBA loan research for Gtano's)
html:
TL;DR

  • April 6, 2020 — one day before the "strange time" monologue — Yahir, age 17, builds a research doc on CARES Act / SBA pandemic-relief loan options for Gtano's's collapse.
  • Bookmarked sources: r/smallbusiness Reddit megathread + an Accountingprose step-by-step on the SBA COVID-19 disaster loan.
  • Catalogued options: PPP (Paycheck Protection Program — forgivable 7(a) loan for retaining employees), EIDL advance ($10k economic injury, must repay), SBA Debt Relief (auto-pays principal/interest/fees on existing 7(a)/504/microloans for 6 months, plus same on new loans pre-Sep-27-2020).
  • This is 17-year-old Yahir running survival research for his dad's business — same texture as the Sept 2019 Craigslist liquidation note but proactive instead of reactive.
  • The bridge income / family-translator role gets explicit here: Yahir is doing the federal-program decoding the household needs.

Why this matters for the graph

  • One half of the April 2020 dad's-house pair with 2020 04 07 what a strange time indeed — same week, same livingroom, same crisis. The relief-loan doc is the operational companion to the macro-thesis monologue.
  • Documents the 17-year-old chief-of-staff role Yahir filled during the Gtano's collapse — financial-research delegate. Pattern reappears in the 2025 Spanish lease template (same admin-labor-as-service mode, scaled up 5 years).
  • Note: PPP and EIDL went on to be massively-fraud-ridden federal programs; whether Yahir/dad ever applied isn't documented in this doc.

Connections
